solutionFiles.content:
from models.book import Book
from models.video import Video
from services.catalog_service import build_descriptions, build_fee_labels

items = [
    Book("Python Patterns", 2023, "A. Lee"),
    Video("Debugging Basics", 2022, 45),
]

for line in build_descriptions(items):
    print(line)

for line in build_fee_labels(items):
    print(line)

# Define build_descriptions(items)
# Define build_fee_labels(items)

solutionFiles.content:
from services.loader import load_items
from services.catalog_service import build_summary

items = load_items("data/items.txt")

for item in items:
    print(item.report_line())

summary = build_summary(items)
print(f"Total items: {summary['count']}")
print(f"Books: {summary['books']}")
print(f"Videos: {summary['videos']}")

from models.book import Book
from models.video import Video


def load_items(file_path):
    items = []
    with open(file_path, "r") as file:
        for raw_line in file:
            line = raw_line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            parts = line.split("|")
            item_type = parts[0]
            title = parts[1]
            year = int(parts[2])

            if item_type == "book":
                author = parts[3]
                items.append(Book(title, year, author))
            elif item_type == "video":
                minutes = int(parts[3])

files.content:
book|Python Patterns|2023|A. Lee
video|Debugging Basics|2022|45
book|Clean APIs|2021|M. Diaz

recipe.solutionCode:
from services.loader import load_items

summary = build_summary(items)
print(f"Total items: {summary['count']}")
print(f"Books: {summary['books']}")
print(f"Videos: {summary['videos']}")

from models.book import Book
from models.video import Video


def load_items(file_path):
    items = []
    with open(file_path, "r") as file:
        for raw_line in file:
            line = raw_line.strip()
            if not line:
                continue
            parts = line.split("|")
            item_type = parts[0]
            title = parts[1]
            year = int(parts[2])

            if item_type == "book":
                author = parts[3]
                items.append(Book(title, year, author))
            elif item_type == "video":
                minutes = int(parts[3])

semanticChecks.message: build_summary(items) should return the total count plus separate book and video counts.
Keep load_items(file_path) working so the final report uses loaded objects.
Print three item report lines and three summary lines in main.py.
